mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real purchasing power for consumers and increases input costs for businesses, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and reduced corporate profitability, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation concerns are high as of 2023-05-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and forward returns becomes positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or coincides with broader economic contractions. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between rising unemployment and recessions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or UNRATE increases are not consistently followed by negative returns within 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are contracting while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is simultaneously rising, it indicates a severe demand-side slowdown exacerbated by restrictive monetary policy. This combination severely impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS growth is negative and FEDFUNDS is increasing, or the interaction term is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During the early stages of an economic expansion, robust growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing manufacturing activity and business investment. This leads to higher corporate revenues and earnings, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production as a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during periods identified as early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer and business confidence, as well as future economic activity across various sectors. This robust demand environment supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a key economic driver
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of periods where HOUST is above its 6-month moving average, or the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds a critical threshold, it signifies a restrictive monetary policy stance aimed at curbing inflation or an overheating economy. This higher cost of capital and reduced liquidity typically leads to lower asset valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS rate is 4.83% as of 2023-04-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or the negative relationship is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy and expanding consumer sector. This strong fundamental demand and employment environment provides a significant tailwind for corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market and consumer spending are pillars of economic growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 40% of periods where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive growth, or the joint effect is not stronger than individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a broad-based economic slowdown, leading to reduced corporate profits and heightened investor uncertainty. This environment consistently drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the primary measure of economic output and recession indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during officially declared recession periods, or the negative correlation is weak.
